summary: Joel shares research findings about stock pump-and-dump spam schemes, citing a study by Frieder and Zittrain showing that investors who buy touted stocks lose about 5.5% while spammers profit nearly 5.8%. He links to Leonard Richardson's site that tracks stock spam activity, confirming these stocks are reliable money-losers. The post then pivots to plugging Leonard's Ruby Cookbook and noting his wife Sumana works at Fog Creek Software.
key_points: Academic research quantifies pump-and-dump spam: buyers lose ~5.5% while spammers earn ~5.79% on average
Stock spam activity can be tracked and charted, making the pattern of losses visible to anyone
The post connects stock spam research to a personal network tie at Fog Creek Software
money_quotes: The average investor who buys a stock on the day it is most heavily touted and sells it 2 days after the touting ends will lose approximately 5.5%.
Buying these scammy penny stocks is a guaranteed way to lose money.
key_insight: Stock pump-and-dump spam is a quantifiably losing proposition for buyers, with research showing spammers profit at roughly the same rate that investors lose.
